concepts.name: Graph
concepts.short_definition: A collection of nodes (vertices) connected by edges.

concepts.name: Undirected Graph
concepts.short_definition: A graph where edges have no direction.

concepts.name: Weighted Graph
concepts.short_definition: A graph where each edge has an associated numerical value (weight).

concepts.name: Connected Graph
concepts.short_definition: A graph where there is a path between any two vertices.

concepts.name: Tree
concepts.short_definition: A connected graph with no cycles.

concepts.name: Spanning Tree
concepts.short_definition: A subgraph that is a tree and connects all vertices of the original graph.

concepts.name: Minimum Spanning Tree
concepts.short_definition: A spanning tree of a weighted, undirected graph with the minimum possible total edge weight.

concepts.name: Edge Weight
concepts.short_definition: The numerical value assigned to an edge in a weighted graph.

concepts.name: Cycle
concepts.short_definition: A path in a graph that starts and ends at the same vertex, without repeating edges.

concepts.name: Cut
concepts.short_definition: A partition of the vertices of a graph into two disjoint sets.

concepts.name: Cut Property
concepts.short_definition: For any cut, the minimum weight edge crossing the cut must be part of some MST.

concepts.name: Kruskal's Algorithm
concepts.short_definition: An algorithm to find the MST by greedily adding edges in increasing order of weight, avoiding cycles.

concepts.name: Prim's Algorithm
concepts.short_definition: An algorithm to find the MST by growing a tree from an arbitrary starting vertex.

concepts.name: Disjoint Set Union
concepts.short_definition: A data structure that tracks a set of elements partitioned into a number of disjoint subsets.

concepts.name: Greedy Algorithm
concepts.short_definition: An algorithm that makes the locally optimal choice at each stage with the hope of finding a global optimum.

concepts.name: Adjacency List
concepts.short_definition: A representation of a graph where each vertex has a list of its adjacent vertices.

concepts.name: Adjacency Matrix
concepts.short_definition: A representation of a graph where a matrix indicates which vertices are adjacent.

concepts.name: Priority Queue
concepts.short_definition: A data structure that stores elements with associated priorities and allows efficient retrieval of the element with the highest priority.

concepts.name: Vertex
concepts.short_definition: A fundamental unit of a graph, representing a point or entity.

concepts.name: Edge
concepts.short_definition: A connection between two vertices in a graph.

concepts.name: Graph Traversal
concepts.short_definition: The process of visiting each vertex in a graph.

concepts.name: Breadth-First Search
concepts.short_definition: A graph traversal algorithm that explores neighbors layer by layer.

concepts.name: Depth-First Search
concepts.short_definition: A graph traversal algorithm that explores as far as possible along each branch before backtracking.

concepts.name: Connected Components
concepts.short_definition: Maximal connected subgraphs of a graph.

concepts.name: Forest
concepts.short_definition: A collection of disjoint trees.

concepts.name: Acyclic Graph
concepts.short_definition: A graph that contains no cycles.

concepts.name: Path
concepts.short_definition: A sequence of vertices connected by edges.

concepts.name: Shortest Path
concepts.short_definition: A path between two vertices with the minimum total edge weight.

concepts.name: Dijkstra's Algorithm
concepts.short_definition: An algorithm to find the shortest paths from a single source vertex to all other vertices in a graph with non-negative edge weights.

concepts.name: Bellman-Ford Algorithm
concepts.short_definition: An algorithm to find the shortest paths from a single source vertex to all other vertices in a graph that may contain negative edge weights.

concepts.name: Graph Representation
concepts.short_definition: Methods for storing and organizing graph data.

concepts.name: Time Complexity
concepts.short_definition: A measure of the amount of time an algorithm takes to run as a function of the input size.

concepts.name: Space Complexity
concepts.short_definition: A measure of the amount of memory an algorithm uses as a function of the input size.

concepts.name: Data Structures
concepts.short_definition: Ways of organizing and storing data for efficient access and modification.

concepts.name: Algorithm Design
concepts.short_definition: The process of creating a step-by-step procedure to solve a problem.

concepts.name: Undirected Edge
concepts.short_definition: An edge that connects two vertices without a specific direction.

concepts.name: Graph Connectivity
concepts.short_definition: Measures of how well-connected the vertices in a graph are.

concepts.name: Edge List
concepts.short_definition: A representation of a graph as a list of all its edges.

concepts.name: Graph Properties
concepts.short_definition: Characteristics that describe a graph, such as being connected, cyclic, or bipartite.
